skill.name: abstract-summarizer
skill.description: Transform lengthy academic papers into concise, structured 250-word abstracts with Background, Objective, Methods, Results, and Conclusion sections.
skill.summary: AI-powered tool that condenses complex research papers into publication-ready structured abstracts while preserving scientific accuracy and key findings.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 90 potential issues, but AI evaluation confirmed 88 of these are false positives from markdown code fences, educational text, and reference links. Two real capabilities exist: network access for fetching papers from user-provided URLs (scripts/main.py:290) and filesystem writes for saving output (scripts/main.py:312). Both are documented, legitimate features of an academic summarization tool. No malicious intent, prompt injection, or data exfiltration patterns detected.

low_findings.title: HTTP requests to user-provided URLs
low_findings.description: The script uses the requests library to fetch content from URLs supplied via the --url parameter. This is a documented feature for retrieving academic papers from web sources. The URL is user-controlled, not hardcoded, and a 30-second timeout is enforced. No evidence of data exfiltration or requests to suspicious endpoints.

low_findings.title: File write to user-specified output path
low_findings.description: The script writes the generated abstract to a file path provided by the user via the --output parameter. This is a documented feature for saving summarization results. The write operation uses UTF-8 encoding and only writes the generated abstract text, not arbitrary content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown code fences flagged as shell backtick execution (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer detected 63 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across SKILL.md and reference markdown files. All are standard markdown code block delimiters (```) used for formatting code examples, templates, and sample abstracts. No actual shell backtick execution exists in any file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Educational use of 'algorithm' flagged as weak cryptography (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 14 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' where the word 'algorithm' appears in educational text about computer science, academic writing templates, and discipline-specific keyword lists. No cryptographic code or hash functions exist anywhere in the skill files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Scientific text flagged as system reconnaissance (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 10 instances of 'System reconnaissance' in educational content about medical research, clinical trial methodology, and abstract evaluation criteria. These are reference materials about academic writing, not host enumeration or system probing commands.

content.user_title: Summarize Academic Papers into Structured Abstracts
content.value_statement: Reading and condensing lengthy academic papers takes hours of researcher time. This skill automatically generates concise 250-word structured abstracts with Background, Objective, Methods, Results, and Conclusion sections while preserving key quantitative data.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured 250-word abstracts from academic papers with Background, Objective, Methods, Results, and Conclusion sections
Extract and preserve quantitative data including sample sizes, p-values, effect sizes, and confidence intervals
Process multiple input formats including PDF files, plain text, and URLs
Adapt summarization strategy by discipline including biomedical, physics, computer science, and social sciences
Batch process multiple papers for systematic literature reviews
Validate word count compliance and section completeness with built-in quality checks
limitations: Requires machine-readable text; cannot interpret scanned images, charts, or graphs embedded in papers
Optimized for English-language empirical research papers; struggles with pure theory papers and non-English content
Papers exceeding 50 pages may need section-by-section processing for complete coverage
Generated abstracts require human review before publication submission to verify numerical accuracy

use_cases.title: Literature Review Acceleration
use_cases.description: Researchers conducting systematic reviews can batch-process dozens of papers into structured abstracts, creating a comparison matrix of findings across studies.

use_cases.title: Journal Submission Preparation
use_cases.description: Authors preparing manuscripts can quickly generate draft abstracts that comply with journal word limits and structured format requirements.

use_cases.title: Research Digest Creation
use_cases.description: Librarians and knowledge managers can summarize newly published papers for institutional research digests and newsletters.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PDF Summarization
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this research paper into a structured 250-word abstract with Background, Objective, Methods, Results, and Conclusion sections. Preserve all statistics and numerical findings from the original text.

prompt_templates.title: Discipline-Specific Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this biomedical research paper into a structured abstract. Focus on study design, sample size, primary endpoint, and statistical significance. Use standard biomedical terminology and preserve all p-values and effect sizes.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Literature Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Process all papers in this directory into structured abstracts. Generate a CSV summary matrix with columns for title, authors, objective, methods, key findings, and effect sizes across all papers.
prompt_templates.scenario: Systematic review preparation with multiple papers

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Cross-Study Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate structured abstracts for these three papers on the same research topic, then create a synthesis document comparing their methods, results, and conclusions. Highlight areas of agreement and contradiction between studies.

output_examples.input: Provide a structured abstract for a paper about a CNN-Transformer hybrid architecture for medical image segmentation evaluated on three public datasets.
output_examples.output:
**Background**: Medical image segmentation faces challenges in capturing both local features and long-range dependencies, with CNNs and Transformers each having limitations.

**Objective**: To develop a hybrid architecture combining CNN local feature extraction with Transformer global context modeling for improved medical image segmentation.

**Methods**: The proposed MedFusion-Transformer uses cross-attention fusion, evaluated on BraTS 2020, LiTS, and ACDC datasets with Dice score metrics and ablation studies.

**Results**: Achieved Dice scores of 0.892 (BraTS), 0.856 (LiTS), and 0.914 (ACDC), outperforming state-of-the-art by 2.3%, 1.8%, and 1.5% respectively while using 40% fewer parameters.

**Conclusion**: Hybrid CNN-Transformer architectures show promise for medical image segmentation, especially in data-scarce settings.

output_examples.input: Generate an abstract for a Phase III clinical trial paper about a novel JAK inhibitor for rheumatoid arthritis patients.
output_examples.output:
**Background**: Rheumatoid arthritis patients with inadequate response to methotrexate need effective alternative treatments.

**Objective**: To evaluate the efficacy and safety of XRZ-789, a novel JAK inhibitor, in patients with moderate-to-severe RA.

**Methods**: Double-blind RCT of 482 patients randomized 2:1 to XRZ-789 200mg daily (n=321) or placebo (n=161) for 24 weeks with background methotrexate. Primary endpoint: ACR20 at week 24.

**Results**: XRZ-789 achieved 68.2% ACR20 response vs 32.9% placebo (p<0.001, 95% CI: 26.8-43.8). ACR50: 42.4% vs 15.5%, ACR70: 23.7% vs 6.2% (both p<0.001). Serious infections: 2.5% vs 1.2%.

**Conclusion**: XRZ-789 demonstrated superior efficacy with a safety profile consistent with other JAK inhibitors, supporting it as a new treatment option for RA patients.

best_practices: Always verify that numbers, statistics, and conclusions in the generated abstract match the original paper before submission
Specify the discipline flag to ensure field-appropriate terminology and correct focus areas for the target domain
Use the quality checklist after generation to confirm all five sections are present and word count is within the 250-word limit
anti_patterns: Do not use generated abstracts for publication without human review and fact-checking of all numerical data against the source paper
Avoid processing papers with heavy mathematical notation or scanned images that the text extractor cannot parse accurately
Do not rely on generated abstracts for papers outside the supported disciplines without verifying terminology and formatting accuracy

faq.question: What types of papers does this skill work best with?
faq.answer: The skill works best with empirical research papers in biomedical sciences, computer science, physics, engineering, and social sciences that have clear section headers.

faq.question: Can this skill handle papers with equations and mathematical notation?
faq.answer: The skill extracts text only. Complex mathematical notation may be lost or misrepresented. Pure theory papers with heavy equations may not summarize accurately.

faq.question: How accurate are the statistics in generated abstracts?
faq.answer: The skill preserves numbers it finds in the text, but always verify against the original paper. Statistical significance indicators and exact values require human confirmation.

faq.question: Can I process scanned PDF documents with this skill?
faq.answer: No, the skill requires machine-readable text. Scanned images or photographs of papers must be converted to text using OCR software first.

faq.question: What is the maximum paper length the skill can handle?
faq.answer: Papers up to 50 pages can be processed in one pass. Longer documents benefit from section-by-section processing for better coverage.

faq.question: Does this skill work with languages other than English?
faq.answer: The skill is optimized for English-language academic papers. Papers in other languages may produce incomplete or inaccurate results.
